name: Dimensional Weight Divisors by Carrier
description: Volumetric (DIM) divisors used to convert shipment volume to billable weight. NOT in the knowledge base; the corpus only defines the concept (volume / minimum density requirement). Values below were verified against carrier-published pages captured in the dated sourcing pack (corpus-logistics-supply-chain/sourced-reference-data-2026-07-02.md). Divisors vary by carrier, service, rate card, country, account agreement, and date — always confirm against the carrier's current service guide before rating freight.
review_frequency: quarterly (carriers revise divisors, often at annual GRI); USPS row needs re-verification on 2026-07-12
source: Checked 2026-07-02 (sourcing pack corpus-logistics-supply-chain/sourced-reference-data-2026-07-02.md): FedEx 'What is dimensional weight?' page (fedex.com/en-us/shipping/packaging/what-is-dimensional-weight.html); UPS 'Shipping Dimensions and Weight' page (ups.com/us/en/support/shipping-support/shipping-dimensions-weight); DHL Express volumetric weight calculator page (dhlexpress.ee/en/terms-and-conditions-2/volumetric-weight-calculator/); USPS Postal Explorer QSG 150 (pe.usps.com/text/qsg300/q150.htm); USPS Federal Register final rule 2026-09785 (federalregister.gov, effective 2026-07-12); IATA TACT convention for the general air-cargo figure.
sourcing_notes: Verified 2026-07-02 against the dated sourcing pack. Confirmed: FedEx official page states 139 in^3/lb for US/Puerto Rico/international packages; UPS official page states 139 in^3/lb for Daily Rates (166 for Retail Rates); DHL Express publishes 5000 cm^3/kg metric (and 139 in^3/lb imperial); USPS Postal Explorer QSG 150 currently shows 166 in^3/lb for qualifying parcels over 1728 in^3. FUTURE CHANGE: a Federal Register final rule moves USPS to 139 in^3/lb with round-up dimension rounding effective 2026-07-12 — re-verify the USPS row on that date. The 6000 cm^3/kg general air-cargo figure is the IATA TACT convention; the pack did not include a dated IATA capture, so that row stays verified: false. Caveat from the pack: carrier DIM divisors are subject to change and may differ by country, service, rate card, tariff, account agreement, and date.

rows.notes: FedEx official page (US/Puerto Rico/international packages) and UPS official page (Daily Rates) both state 139, checked 2026-07-02. UPS Retail Rates use 166 instead. DHL Express also publishes 139 in^3/lb imperial.

USPS Postal Explorer QSG 150 (parcels over 1728 in^3) and UPS Retail Rates both state 166, checked 2026-07-02. USPS CHANGES TO 139 EFFECTIVE 2026-07-12 per Federal Register final rule (future-effective as of the pack date) — re-verify this row then.
IATA TACT convention (equivalent to 167 kg/m^3). The 2026-07-02 sourcing pack did not include a dated IATA capture, so this row is the industry convention, not a carrier-verified value.

DHL Express publishes 5000 cm^3/kg (volumetric weight calculator page, checked 2026-07-02; DHL Discover pages agree). FedEx China's metric DIM page also states 5000. Confirm the local country tariff before rating.
